title: Osteoporosis endocrine follow-up

investigations.text: Defines Abu Dhabi primary-care osteoporosis screening scope, primarily for adults over 50, with follow-up and treatment-effect monitoring context.
investigations.rationale: The exact UAE scope and follow-up context supports patient goals, clinician-entered plan and arranged follow-up without choosing treatment.
investigations.evidence_extract: Defines Abu Dhabi primary-care osteoporosis screening scope, primarily for adults over 50, with follow-up and treatment-effect monitoring context.

source.title: DOH Guidelines on Screening for Osteoporosis

treatment_and_management.text: Supports detailed risk history, fragility-fracture context, falls risk, FRAX and BMD context, and monitoring of treatment effects.
treatment_and_management.rationale: The exact DoH fracture-risk section supports interval history, functional impact, associated or negative symptom context, measured weight and clinician-reviewed risk context without calculating FRAX or assigning a diagnosis.
treatment_and_management.evidence_extract: Supports detailed risk history, fragility-fracture context, falls risk, FRAX and BMD context, and monitoring of treatment effects.

source.title: DOH Guidelines on Screening for Osteoporosis

referral.text: Supports clinician-reviewed DEXA reporting with age and risk qualifiers and without independently assigning a diagnosis.
referral.rationale: The exact DoH BMD section supports review of already ordered endocrine or bone-density results and existing specialist correspondence without independently interpreting DEXA.
referral.evidence_extract: Supports clinician-reviewed DEXA reporting with age and risk qualifiers and without independently assigning a diagnosis.

source.title: DOH Guidelines on Screening for Osteoporosis

source_limitations: Fracture and falls history, DEXA values, FRAX variables, secondary causes, medicines, adherence, adverse effects, diagnosis and treatment remain unsupported.
Any legacy item not explicitly mapped to an exact section remains unsupported pending qualified clinician review.
No mapping authorises an automatic diagnosis, differential, investigation, management decision, medicine, dose, procedure, referral, urgency conclusion, discharge decision, or patient instruction.
All prior supported legacy-item mappings were removed because canonical item-level applicability and workflow-specific rationale were not present in explicit repository records.
curation_policy: Direct guideline curation: retain only explicitly mapped content; rewrite only with explicit item mapping; remove unmapped legacy content; add only committed exact-section evidence summaries.
